title: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN
display_title: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN
wikitext:
[]: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by boyfrand » Tue Mar 23, 2021 3:11 pm Before any of y'all come up in here and remind me that the Eva lore isn't the main thing about Eva, keep in mind we're on a forum with a very extensive Wiki attached to it. So clearly a lot of people here do care about the lore. Personally, I watched NGE a few times before finding Evageeks. I came to figure out what the heck was going on with some parts, and then stayed for Reichu's analysis of the lore - I'm sure a lot of others here have similar experiences and interest in the lore. Also, I don't deeply relate to any of the characters - I actually wouldn't tolerate any person who has any of their personalities in real life to be honest. So if you want to post about the lore not being important, please save it for a different thread - this one isn't for you, it's for people interested in the lore. In NGE and EOE, I thought the development of the lore was great for building mystery (whether it was intentional or not), and for what they do reveal, the revelation is usually awesome. Yui in Eva 01, the Marduk Institute and the classmates, Misato's character development after finding out she was lied to about Adam, and a few other examples. I thought the extra-canon stuff from the video games about the FAR was cool and really would have liked to see it incorporated into Rebuild in some way. From 1.0 to 3.0, it really "felt like" NTE was going to try to do a better job at explaining the lore. The main reasons it felt that way were the allusions to NGE, Misato calling the basement creature Lilith upfront, and a seemingly expanded (but still unexplained) lore. But ultimately, each film introduced new elements that needed to be explained in the subsequent films (and then seemingly, a lot was left unexplained). It would have been cool to learn more about the SDSS, what the heck all the new units are (still no idea why Unit 5 was what it was and that's just the beginning of mysteries with new units), what's a "true evangelion," etc. But after reading about the finale of SHIN and finding out about Kaworu looping through different Eva cycles, does any of it matter at all? Finding out a Kaworu wakes up in an Eva-verse and the rules are just similar and can kind of be very whatever really deflated my interest in actually understanding the backstory and scifi-fantasy plot of NTE. Why would I care about the different Eva 4's, what unit 6 seconds actually is, whatever is going with the DSS choker, etc., if it's presented in a way that those are just incidental details because Shinji needs to learn how to be happy or be well adjusted or something. There are so many cool or unusual things in NTE that need some kind of explanation (which they don't get anyway), but I no longer see a reason to pour over the dialogue and visual details to unpack the lore when it's all impermanent by the lore's own standards. TL;DR: How do you feel about the lore now that we know Eva is just a bunch of unconnected cycles or whatever?

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Raikyu » Tue Mar 23, 2021 4:01 pm I think that a real discussion about the lore of NTE will only be possible after everyone in EvaGeeks is able to watch it. The only information we were able to obtain came from the interpretation of some people, and others may have different interpretations. It is possible that a hypothetic 3.33+1.11 Blu-Ray/DVD release may add new scenes to fill some gaps. Besides the questions you listed, another thing that intrigues me is which angelic being is the basis of each individual Evangelion. In the original show, we know that every unit is a clone of Adam except Unit-01 which is a clone of Lilith. In NTE, from unit-00 to unit-08 we have no clue, but we know that units 09-12 are the four Adams (or its cores?) and unit-13 is "one of the surviving Adams" (maybe a central fifth Adam?) Another mystery is the role of Kaworu. Is he still related to Adam/Adams? There is definitely a link between Kaworu, Eva-13, and the Key. Mark 6 initially seemed to have a link with the Adams too, but in the end, it didn't have any particular purpose besides merging with Lilith. Unless it is a clone of Lilith like Unit-01. I don't know. There are a lot of mysteries and I think that we are unable to solve them at the moment.

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 23, 2021 4:10 pm I wonder if they gave up on it or if it just seems that way... Ultimately we all came for the characters & the gross horror stuff but inconsistency always irked me. Was Gendo's explanation about the "options left to us by the gods" supposed to be the standin for Misato's angel infodump in EoE? I cannot decide yet wether or not it's really more opaque than the OG was, I feel it was more consistent but that's easy to say after we got all the glossary booklets & stuff like the CI. Precursors were explicitly confirmed onscreen (whereas the closest we got in ep 21 is hearing the geofront is artificial) There's also a line about the total number of spears - 6 I think? A lot of stuff like the pillars etc. was clearly implied to be mysterious stuff left behind by precursors. IDK if the 'Adams' were also excavated or produced at second impact.

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by boyfrand » Tue Mar 23, 2021 8:43 pm Ha thanks guys - your further questions about the lore reignited my interest in SHIN a little bit, so thank you. I’ll definitely have to see for myself how the Kaworu looping aspect is handled before I can say it really has diminished my interest in Eva. Now I’m also wondering if the limited lore is some kind of marketing ploy. Anno and team must know on some level that people have continuously speculated about Eva lore since the beginning (some of y’all here put in the time and work equivalent to an Eva PhD and I mean that as a compliment). It could be intentional this time to keep fans engaged over the long term by speculating over lore in fanfics and fan art. If you’re always thinking about Eva, you’re more likely to buy more merch and special edition home releases!

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Mar 23, 2021 8:43 pm Raikyu wrote: I think that a real discussion about the lore of NTE will only be possible after everyone in EvaGeeks is able to watch it.

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Raikyu » Tue Mar 23, 2021 9:24 pm Axx°N N. wrote: Yep. I feel less confident commenting/interpreting/debating the lore than the character writing or shape of the plot. Eva lore has always relied on picking apart imagery and even freeze-frames. How long did it take for everyone to realize the entry plugs of the MPEs in EoE read "Kaworu" for instance? Heck, that might even still be new info for some.

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Mar 23, 2021 10:11 pm Raikyu wrote: One thing that I forgot to mention about the questions I have regarding the lore is what was the role of Lilith in SEELE's or Gendo's plan? In the original show, Lilith was a crucial actor in the instrumentality (or a clone of Lilith in SEELE's scenario). But from what I've read, Lilith seemed to lose some importance.

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Mar 24, 2021 2:21 am Axx°N N. wrote: Eva lore has always relied on picking apart imagery and even freeze-frames. How long did it take for everyone to realize the entry plugs of the MPEs in EoE read "Kaworu" for instance? Heck, that might even still be new info for some.

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Kid » Wed Mar 24, 2021 10:52 pm I am super interested in the lore! I'm very happy 3.0+1.0 didn't shy away from radical sci-fi concepts, because one of my favorite things about the series is the "metaphysical biology" element and how it connects to so much of the stuff people would dismiss as magic nonsense. Even Kaworu's cycle/loop/Book of Life nonsense can be connected to it. It's hokey science of course, but damn do I love it and DAMN did Thrice deliver! No true EvaGeek would seriously discuss lore without seeing the film, so that's the only ONLY thing holding me back from having lore discussions on every forum, image board, and social media DM

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Mar 25, 2021 12:21 am Mr. Tines wrote: As for freeze-frames, taken too far, that approach also led to the notorious "Tree of Life at Second Impact" red herring. By contrast, I found the "Kaworu" on the MPE dummy plug obvious on a first viewing.

[]: Re: How do you feel about the lore after SHIN?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 25, 2021 2:45 am Axx°N N. wrote: As for me, if you can believe it, the dummy plug detail took me about a decade. Like many, I first watched EoE on a badly optimized tube TV via the badly mastered western DVD, so I don't even have a clear memory of reacting to Shinji's goo hand from my first view.

So clearly a lot of people here do care about the lore. Personally, I watched NGE a few times before finding Evageeks. I came to figure out what the heck was going on with some parts, and then stayed for Reichu's analysis of the lore - I'm sure a lot of others here have similar experiences and interest in the lore. Also, I don't deeply relate to any of the characters - I actually wouldn't tolerate any person who has any of their personalities in real life to be honest. So if you want to post about the lore not being important, please save it for a different thread - this one isn't for you, it's for people interested in the lore. In NGE and EOE, I thought the development of the lore was great for building mystery (whether it was intentional or not), and for what they do reveal, the revelation is usually awesome. Yui in Eva 01, the Marduk Institute and the classmates, Misato's character development after finding out she was lied to about Adam, and a few other examples.
